log_entries.thought_preview: The sample overview confirms this is a PE32 executable tagged as 'corrupt', 'spreader', and with an 'overlay'. The file is 53KB. I've already seen network activity, capabilities, and some artifacts. N
The IOC summary shows 2 unique IPs, 1 domain, 1 dropped file, and 1 process created. No persistence keys were explicitly listed, but the registry changes showed HexStream modifications which might be

The dropped file is the same executable (hhcbrnaff.exe) that was executed, placed in the Temp folder. This suggests the malware might be self-extracting or the initial sample is the payload. I need to

The registry changes tool returned empty results, but the initial overview showed HexStream modifications and Internet Settings changes. This discrepancy might be due to how the tool categorizes keys.

The network activity confirms the malware connects to glyphs-design.com (173.231.184.55:443) and uses Google DNS (8.8.8.8). No HTTP conversations were captured, suggesting the connection might be HTTP

The process activity shows only hhcbrnaff.exe was created with no notable modules or highlighted API calls, which is consistent with the initial overview showing GetProcAddress as the only dangerous i

They are behavior descriptions, not ATT&CK IDs. Map them only if you know the real MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise technique ID.

- Do not invent placeholder IDs such as T1234.
- Remove TTPs that are not directly justified by the report.
